pico.population: Large Language Models (autoregressive transformer-based), including both open-weight models (LLaMA, Mistral, Qwen) and API-only/black-box models (GPT-4, Claude, Gemini)
pico.intervention: Uncertainty quantification methods for generation: semantic entropy, verbalized confidence (self-verbalized uncertainty), P(True)/self-evaluation, consistency-based sampling (SelfCheckGPT), ensemble/MC-dropout, attention-based signals, length-normalized likelihood, token-to-sequence aggregation
pico.comparison: Baseline likelihood/perplexity methods; temperature-scaling calibration; white-box (logit-accessible) vs black-box (API-only) method families; hallucination-detection baselines
pico.outcome: Calibration quality (Expected Calibration Error, Brier score); selective prediction performance (AUROC for misclassification detection, risk-coverage curve, selective risk); hallucination detection accuracy; epistemic vs aleatoric decomposition validity
pico.setting: Short-form QA (TriviaQA, NaturalQuestions, SQuAD, TruthfulQA); multi-choice (MMLU); reasoning (GSM8K, MATH, chain-of-thought); long-form generation (HaluEval, FActScore); emerging: code generation, multilingual, agentic/tool-use

user_interests.priority_direction: Attention-based UQ (using attention patterns as epistemic signal) — user's original angle

seed_papers: Kadavath et al. 2022 — Language Models (Mostly) Know What They Know
Farquhar et al. 2024 (Nature) — Detecting hallucinations via semantic entropy
Lin et al. 2022 — Teaching Models to Express Uncertainty in Words
Kuhn et al. 2023 — Semantic Uncertainty: Linguistic Invariances
Xiong et al. 2024 (ICLR) — Can LLMs Express Their Uncertainty?
Tian et al. 2023 — Just Ask for Calibration
Duan et al. 2024 — Shifting Attention to Relevance (SAR)
Geng et al. 2024 — Survey of Confidence Estimation and Calibration in LLMs
Huang et al. 2024 — Survey on UQ Methods for Deep Learning

queries.query: ("large language model" OR LLM) AND ("uncertainty quantification" OR "confidence calibration" OR "selective prediction")
queries.rationale: Direct hit on the primary topic — captures papers explicitly framing their work as UQ/calibration/selective prediction for LLMs

queries.query: ("semantic entropy" OR "semantic uncertainty" OR "verbalized confidence" OR "P(True)" OR "self-evaluation" OR "consistency-based") AND (LLM OR "language model" OR generation)
queries.rationale: Catches papers that use specific method names rather than the umbrella term 'UQ' — essential since SOTA papers (Farquhar, Kadavath, Lin) often foreground the method name

queries.query: ("epistemic uncertainty" OR "aleatoric uncertainty") AND ("language model" OR autoregressive OR "next-token") AND (logits OR likelihood OR perplexity)
queries.rationale: Finds the theoretical grounding — epistemic/aleatoric decomposition, likelihood-based signals, token-level probability mechanisms underlying LLM UQ

queries.query: ("Expected Calibration Error" OR ECE OR "risk-coverage" OR "AUROC") AND LLM AND (TriviaQA OR MMLU OR TruthfulQA OR GSM8K OR HaluEval OR FActScore)
queries.rationale: Targets empirical/benchmark papers — finds works with rigorous evaluation on standard UQ benchmarks, useful for baseline comparison and reproducibility

queries.query: ("attention-based" OR "attention pattern" OR "chain-of-thought" OR "tool-use" OR agentic OR "long-form generation") AND (uncertainty OR confidence OR hallucination) AND (LLM OR transformer)
queries.rationale: Targets the user's priority direction (attention-based UQ) plus emerging frontiers (CoT-step UQ, agentic UQ, long-form UQ) where literature is sparse but differentiation is high
